message:
chore(beads): Close bd-1dt84 Budget & Resource Control feature

All core components implemented and tested:
- BudgetTracker: per-agent/total tracking with enforcement
- EarlyStopDetector: findings rate and similarity thresholds
- Token estimation via internal/tokens
- ContextPackCache with TTL and invalidation
- EarlyStop wired into pipeline stage2

TUI dashboard deferred to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Edge Cases

- Negative spend (error)
- Budget exceeded mid-agent
- Early stop triggers synthesis

## Logging Requirements
- Log budget state changes
- Log early stop evaluations
- Log threshold comparisons

 {"id":"bd-10qa5","title":"CAAM: Add [integrations.caam] config section to TOML","description":"Add configuration support for CAAM integration in NTM config file.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.caam]
# Enable CAAM account management
enabled = true

# Path to caam binary (optional, defaults to PATH lookup)
binary_path = ""

# Enable automatic account rotation on rate limit
auto_rotate = true

# Providers to manage (empty = all available)
providers = ["claude", "openai", "gemini"]

# Rate limit detection patterns (in addition to defaults)
rate_limit_patterns = []

# Cooldown before retrying same account (seconds)
account_cooldown = 300

# Alert threshold (percentage of limit)
alert_threshold = 80
```

## Implementation Location
- Update: internal/config/config.go (add CAAMConfig struct)
- Update: internal/config/defaults.go (set defaults)

## Struct Definition

```go
type CAAMConfig struct {
    Enabled           bool     `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath        string   `toml:"binary_path"`
    AutoRotate        bool     `toml:"auto_rotate"`
    Providers         []string `toml:"providers"`
    RateLimitPatterns []string `toml:"rate_limit_patterns"`
    AccountCooldown   int      `toml:"account_cooldown"`
    AlertThreshold    int      `toml:"alert_threshold"`
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:49:30.659313954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:09:57.907305322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:09:53.391877677Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10qa5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:49:30.731389875Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-10ufs","title":"EPIC: Fleet Management Commands (scale, rebalance, review-queue)","description":"## Goal
Provide a cohesive set of commands for managing agent fleet composition, workload distribution, and batch operations.

## Commands in Scope

### ntm scale
Manual scaling to target agent counts without autonomous auto-scaling.
- `ntm scale <session> --cc=N --cod=N --gmi=N`
- Computes delta vs current counts and calls ntm add/kill
- Safe confirmations for scale-down
- Never auto-kills user pane

### ntm rebalance
Manual rebalance suggestions when agents join/leave or workloads become uneven.
- `ntm rebalance <session>` shows imbalance and suggests reassignments
- `ntm rebalance <session> --apply` applies after confirmation
- Detects dead agents and suggests orphan task reassignment

### ntm review-queue
Suggests review prompts for idle agents to keep them productive.
- `ntm review-queue <session>` lists idle agents + recommended prompts
- `ntm review-queue <session> --send` dispatches after confirmation
- Configurable idle threshold and review templates

### ntm send --batch
Explicit batch prompt delivery from files.
- `ntm send <session> --batch file.txt` sends prompts in order
- Supports comments (#) and separators (---) in batch files
- Agent targeting: --filter, --agent, --broadcast

## Design Philosophy
- All commands are **manual** (user-initiated), not autonomous
- All modifications require **confirmation** (unless --force)
- All commands support **--dry-run** for safe preview
- All commands support **--format json** for robot mode
- Graceful degradation when assignment tracking unavailable

## Success Criteria
- Commands work without assignment tracking (basic mode)
- Commands enhance with assignment tracking when available
- Clear, deterministic output for same input state
- No background daemons or automatic triggers
- Comprehensive logging for debugging","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-11mtt","title":"Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset","description":"# Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset

## Purpose
Ship the **complete 80-mode taxonomy** from `modes_of_reasoning.md` in `internal/ensemble/modes.go`, with tiered visibility for UX filtering. The catalog must be fully usable without external files.

- **core tier (~28)**: curated default set for UX
- **advanced tier (remaining modes)**: hidden by default but fully accessible via `--all` / `--tier`

## Core Tier (Curated 28)

> These are `tier: core` and must have complete metadata + hand-authored preambles.
> **Mode codes and categories must match the numbering in `modes_of_reasoning.md` (A–L).**
> The groupings below are advisory; final category assignment must follow the doc.

- deductive — Deductive Inference
- mathematical-proof — Mathematical Proof
- formal-verification — Formal Verification
- type-theoretic — Type-Theoretic Reasoning
- inductive — Inductive Generalization
- conceptual-blending — Conceptual Blending
- analogical — Analogical Transfer
- ambiguity-detection — Ambiguity Detection
- edge-case — Edge Case Reasoning
- prototype-reasoning — Prototype Reasoning
- root-cause — Root Cause Analysis
- dependency-mapping — Dependency Mapping
- argument-mapping — Argument Mapping
- adversarial-review — Adversarial Review
- systems-thinking — Systems Thinking
- worst-case — Worst-Case Analysis
- decision-under-uncertainty — Decision Under Uncertainty
- failure-mode — Failure Mode Analysis
- second-order-effects — Second-Order Effects
- resource-allocation — Resource Allocation
- strategic-planning — Strategic Planning
- option-generation — Option Generation
- prioritization — Prioritization
- engineering-design — Engineering Design Reasoning
- test-plan — Test Plan Mode
- compliance — Compliance Lens
- meta-cognitive — Meta-cognitive Monitoring
- perspective-taking — Perspective Taking

## Advanced Tier (Remaining 52)

- All remaining modes from `modes_of_reasoning.md` are included as `tier: advanced`.
- Categories must remain within A–L taxonomy.
- ModeCode **letter must match category** (A→Formal, B→Ampliative, ... L→Meta).

## Implementation Pattern
```go
var EmbeddedModes = []ReasoningMode{
    {
        ID:        "deductive",
        Code:      "A1",
        Tier:      TierCore,
        Name:      "Deductive Inference",
        Category:  CategoryFormal,
        ShortDesc: "Classical logical inference",
        // ...
    },
    // ... all 80 modes
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **All 80 modes** defined with complete metadata
- [ ] Core tier is exactly the curated 28 list above
- [ ] Advanced modes included and validated (no feature loss)
- [ ] Codes unique and category letters correct
- [ ] Categories limited to A–L taxonomy
- [ ] Modes validated at init time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:09.088788198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:01:02.638087733Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T23:01:02.638023412Z","close_reason":"Implemented full 80-mode embedded catalog in modes.go with 28 core + 52 advanced tier modes. All modes mapped to A-L taxonomy codes. DefaultCatalog() helper and CatalogVersion constant added. 11 comprehensive tests verify: count, tier distribution, validation, uniqueness, category-letter consistency, all categories present, code-to-ID lookup, and ListDefault correctness.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:33:01.350316238Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:33:43.770208854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11y0m","title":"LimitDetector: Capture pane output via tmux capture-pane","description":"## LimitDetector: Capture Pane Output via tmux capture-pane

### Purpose
Implement the pane output capture functionality that feeds pattern matching.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Function:**
```go
// capturePaneOutput captures the last N lines from a pane.
func (d *LimitDetector) capturePaneOutput(sessionPane string, lines int) (string, error) {
    // sessionPane format: "session_name:window.pane" (e.g., "cc_agents_1:1.3")
    
    // Use /usr/bin/tmux to avoid zsh plugin issues
    args := []string{
        "capture-pane",
        "-t", sessionPane,
        "-p",                          // Print to stdout
        fmt.Sprintf("-S", "-%d", lines), // Start line (negative = from end)
    }
    
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "/usr/bin/tmux", args...)
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("capture-pane failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    return string(output), nil
}
```

**Integration with Existing Code:**
The `internal/tmux/session.go` already has capture-pane functionality. Reuse that:
```go
output, err := tmux.CapturePaneOutput(sessionPane, 50)
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log capture: `[LimitDetector] capture_pane session_pane=%s lines=%d bytes=%d`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capture last N lines from pane
- [ ] Proper session:window.pane addressing
- [ ] Use /usr/bin/tmux directly
- [ ] Handle capture failures gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:54.713444511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:17.427395555Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:17.427347334Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11y0m","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.392125380Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11y0m","depends_on_id":"bd-rt9vb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.149105820Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1dfip","title":"Implement verification logic for restart phases","description":"Implement verification logic for each restart phase.

## Verification Points

### 1. Exit Verification
Check pane output for shell prompt return.
Look for: bash/zsh prompt, $ or >, username@hostname pattern.

### 2. Launch Verification  
Check for claude code ready indicators.
Look for: banner text, prompt character, ready state.

### 3. Prompt Delivery Verification
Verify prompt was received.
Look for: echo of sent text in pane output.

## Implementation
Each verification should:
- Poll with reasonable timeout
- Return detailed status (not just bool)
- Capture relevant output for debugging

## --no-verify Flag
Skip verification for faster operation (less safe).
Use when operator knows environment is stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:37.143997287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:53.699602668Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:53.699451924Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dfip","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:37.172679661Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dmet","title":"Task: Implement output capture for ensemble agents in internal/ensemble/capture.go","description":"# Task: Implement Output Capture for Ensemble Agents

## Location
internal/ensemble/capture.go

## Purpose
Capture pane output from each agent in the ensemble and extract the **YAML output schema** for synthesis. Must handle large outputs and malformed YAML gracefully.

## Implementation
```go
package ensemble

type CapturedOutput struct {
    ModeID        string
    PaneName      string
    RawOutput     string
    Parsed        *ModeOutput
    ParseErrors   []error
    CapturedAt    time.Time
    LineCount     int
    TokenEstimate int
}

type OutputCapture struct {
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    maxLines   int  // Default 1000
    validator  *SchemaValidator
}

func (c *OutputCapture) CaptureAll(session *EnsembleSession) ([]CapturedOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) capturePane(pane string) (string, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) extractYAML(raw string) (string, bool)
```

## YAML Extraction Strategy
- Prefer first fenced ```yaml block
- Fallback: detect `thesis:` root line and capture until EOF
- If multiple YAML blocks, choose the largest valid parse

## Integration with Synthesis
- Parsed ModeOutput is passed to OutputCollector
- RawOutput retained for debugging
- Token estimates via internal/tokens package
- **Do not fail if mode_id missing**; OutputCollector will inject from assignment metadata via Normalize

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Captures output from all ensemble panes
- [ ] Extracts YAML schema blocks reliably
- [ ] Handles missing/malformed output gracefully
- [ ] Token estimation for synthesis planning
- [ ] Configurable max lines to capture
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + parse outcomes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:44.994035314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170149202Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170130136Z","close_reason":"Implemented output capture and extraction logic","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dmet","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:58:17.063829813Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T20:06:14.857050962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:09:24.008880566Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.268189513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:09:24.008880566Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.268189513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1e5jh","title":"Task: Add tab completion for ensemble commands (modes, presets, status, synthesize)","description":"# Task: Tab Completion for Ensemble Commands

Update shell completions to include the ensemble command family and its subcommands.

## Commands to Include
- ntm ensemble list
- ntm ensemble spawn
- ntm ensemble modes
- ntm ensemble presets
- ntm ensemble status
- ntm ensemble synthesize

## Dynamic Completions
- Preset names from built-in + user presets
- Mode IDs from catalog
- Session names (from active tmux sessions)

## Flag Completions
- `--tier` values: core|advanced|experimental|all
- `--allow-advanced` (boolean)

## Files / Locations
- internal/cli/init.go (completion script generator)
- internal/cli/completion_helpers.go (dynamic sources)
- internal/cli/cli_test.go or internal/cli/init_test.go (completion tests)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Completion script includes all ensemble subcommands
- Dynamic completions for presets/modes are non-empty
- New tier/advanced flags included
- Tests updated to cover new completion entries","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:15:36.019385618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T10:26:15.861118469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e5jh","depends_on_id":"bd-221kf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:16:01.588490422Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e5jh","depends_on_id":"bd-28o9m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:15:42.697857721Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e5jh","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0qv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:15:56.923848344Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e5jh","depends_on_id":"bd-6mls6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:15:47.340535080Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e5jh","depends_on_id":"bd-aklq2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:15:36.047891150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e5jh","depends_on_id":"bd-gcee1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:15:52.330194381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ed7","title":"E2E Tests: Full session recovery workflow","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Session Recovery

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Clean session save/restore**
   - Spawn session with 3 agents
   - Execute some commands
   - Kill session (simulate crash)
   - Run ntm recover
   - Verify all panes restored with scrollback

2. **Partial recovery**
   - Spawn session
   - Corrupt one pane's state file
   - Recover
   - Verify remaining panes restored
   - Verify error reported for corrupted pane

3. **Recovery after long idle**
   - Spawn session
   - Wait 30+ seconds
   - Kill and recover
   - Verify state still valid

4. **Multi-project recovery**
   - Spawn sessions for 2 projects
   - Kill both
   - Recover each independently

### Detailed Logging
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Spawning session: %d panes", numPanes)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] State captured: %d bytes", stateSize)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Simulating crash...")
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Restoring pane %d/%d: %s", i, total, paneName)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Scrollback match: %v (%d lines)", match, lines)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:50.783368496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380086093Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380006103Z","close_reason":"E2E test suite implemented with 5 comprehensive tests: TestFullSessionRecovery (full spawn/crash/recover cycle), TestPartialRecovery (corruption handling), TestRecoveryAfterLongIdle (idle period resilience), TestMultiProjectRecovery (cross-project recovery), TestRecoveryContextBuilding (context validation). All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:50.805501324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-3law","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:19:18.495604297Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
